item.title: Egress firewall (iptables CLAWHQ_FWD chain)
item.intent: Enforce that no egress traffic leaves the agent container except to explicitly allowlisted domains. The privacy promise is architectural, not advisory.
item.description: PRIVACY PROMISE: The egress firewall is what makes 'zero data leaves your machine by default' true, not marketing. Without it, an agent tool or skill could call out to arbitrary domains. Per-integration domain allowlist means the user's email agent can reach email APIs and nothing else. Air-gap mode is the paranoid user's kill switch. Acceptance: docker bridge traffic blocked to non-allowlisted domains; DNS and HTTPS to allowlist work; firewall reapplied after compose down; 'clawhq doctor' verifies rules are active.
steps: Create dedicated iptables chain CLAWHQ_FWD: ESTABLISHED/RELATED -> DNS -> HTTPS allowlist -> LOG + DROP
Derive per-integration domain allowlist from config
Add verification with expected vs actual rule diff
Implement auto-reapply after docker compose down
acceptanceCriteria: Docker bridge traffic blocked to non-allowlisted domains
DNS and HTTPS to allowlisted domains work correctly
Firewall reapplied automatically after compose down
'clawhq doctor' verifies firewall rules are active
